summary: Matched the stage function grCastle_801CDFD8 by simplifying its C in src/melee/gr/grcastle.c. The PR body says this was a minor cleanup that moved the function from a 99.84% match to 100%. The diff removed redundant temporaries for params, constants, base value, and final_value, replacing them with direct grCs_804D6970 field reads and literal assignments.

key_files.reason: Only changed file; contains grCastle_801CDFD8 and the matching cleanup.

what_changed: In grCastle_801CDFD8, removed extra grCastleParams* locals named params and params2 and read grCs_804D6970->xA / grCs_804D6970->x8 directly.
Renamed the random bound local from random_range to range.
Kept the existing zero-range guard: HSD_Randi(range) is called only when range != 0, otherwise rand_result is set to 0.
Removed temporary locals neg_one, zero, base_value, and final_value.
Assigned gp->gv.castle9.xD4 directly from grCs_804D6970->x8 + rand_result instead of using an explicit s16 final_value temporary.
Assigned -1 and 0 literals directly to gp->gv.castle9.xDC, xDA, xD8, and xD6.
Preserved the behavior that sets gp->gv.castle9.xDE_b0 = true.
smart_moves: Reduced artificial local-variable pressure, which likely improved register allocation or instruction scheduling enough to close the final 0.16% mismatch.
Used direct global struct field access instead of staging grCs_804D6970 through multiple pointer locals.
Let the destination field assignment handle narrowing for xD4 rather than forcing an explicit s16 temporary.
Used immediate constants for -1 and 0 rather than named integer locals, aligning the C more closely with likely compiler output.
decomp_lessons: For near-matching functions, redundant temporaries can be the mismatch source even when the C behavior is unchanged.
When a struct field assignment has the intended target type, an explicit cast-through temporary may produce different codegen than direct assignment.
Duplicate pointer locals to the same global config object can hurt matching; try direct global field reads if the original compiler likely emitted them.
Literal stores such as -1 and 0 may match better than initializing locals and reusing them.
Small cleanup-only PRs can be valuable when they convert a high-percent match into a full match.
naming_conventions: Stage function naming keeps the grCastle_ address form, here grCastle_801CDFD8.
Castle stage globals use the grCs_ prefix, as in grCs_804D6970.
Ground state access follows gp->gv.castle9 field naming with offset-style members such as xD4, xD6, xD8, xDA, xDC, and bitfield-style xDE_b0.
The simplified local name range is preferred over the more verbose random_range in this matching context.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Remove unnecessary aliases to globals when matching compiler output.
Try direct expression assignment instead of explicit temporary variables for arithmetic feeding a struct field.
Replace constant locals with immediate literal assignments when stores are simple.
Keep control flow minimal around random helpers: range != 0 ? HSD_Randi(range) : 0.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR context.
The only human text says this was a minor cleanup taking grCastle_801CDFD8 from 99.84% to 100% matched.
follow_up_queries: Find other near-matching stage functions with redundant params/params2 aliases to global stage parameter structs.
Search for explicit s16 final_value temporaries assigned into s16 struct fields that might be removable for matching.
Search grcastle.c for constant locals named zero or neg_one used only for simple field stores.
Review other grCs_804D6970 users for direct field access versus local pointer alias patterns.
